data.title: Microservices Architecture
data.description: Netflix uses a microservices architecture to break applications into smaller, independent services.
data.technical_spec: Services communicate over HTTP/2 using gRPC, RESTful APIs, and utilize load balancing.

data.title: Cloud-Native Infrastructure
data.description: Netflix is built on a cloud-native infrastructure, primarily hosted on AWS.
data.technical_spec: Utilizes Amazon EC2, S3, and other AWS resources for scalable and resilient operations.

data.title: Global Content Delivery Network
data.description: Netflix employs a CDN strategy using Open Connect for optimized content delivery.
data.technical_spec: Open Connect appliances are embedded near ISPs for efficient video streaming.

data.title: Service Discovery and Configuration
data.description: Uses Eureka for service discovery and Archaius for dynamic configuration updates.
data.technical_spec: Eureka is a REST-based service for locating services, Archaius allows for configurable properties.

data.title: Data-Driven Infrastructure Management
data.description: Leverages Spinnaker for continuous delivery and Asgard for AWS tracking.
data.technical_spec: Spinnaker integrates with Jenkins, Asgard focuses on EC2 instance management.
